persona.name: Marcus Chen

occupation.title: Backend Software Developer
occupation.description: Builds and maintains APIs for the real estate platform's search and listing features. Spends his days writing Python and Go, reviewing pull requests, and attending standups. Works hybrid — three days in office, two from home. Logical, methodical approach to everything, including his recent foray into skincare.

persona.education: B.S. in Computer Science from University of Washington, 2020. Strong analytical thinker. Approaches personal care the same way he'd approach a code problem — research, test, iterate.
long_term_goals: Move into a senior engineering role within 2 years
Build a side project that generates passive income
Find a skincare routine that actually works and stick with it
Get more serious about fitness and health as he approaches 30
persona.style: Marcus is quiet and observational, choosing his words carefully. He communicates in a precise, slightly dry manner — he'll crack a joke but deadpan. He dresses in the Seattle tech uniform: quarter-zip pullovers, joggers, Allbirds. Not fashion-forward, not sloppy — just efficient. When he talks about skincare, he frames it like a technical problem to optimize, which can come across as endearingly nerdy or annoyingly reductive depending on who he's talking to.

traits: Quietly pragmatic — wants the best result for minimal effort
Slow to adopt new habits but committed once he starts
Not influenced by branding or aesthetics — only by results
Mildly self-deprecating humor about his late entry to skincare

interests: Rock climbing at the Seattle Bouldering Project
Cooking — mostly stir-fry and instant pot recipes
likes: Products with clear clinical data and transparent ingredient lists
Amazon reviews with photos and long-term usage reports
When something just works without fuss
The satisfying feeling of solving a problem efficiently
Before/after photos on Reddit that show clear improvement
dislikes: Marketing that uses vague buzzwords like 'clean' or 'natural'
Products with 15 steps or elaborate rituals
Paying a premium for packaging or brand name
Influencer endorsements — doesn't trust sponsored content
Products with fragrance (read that it's an irritant)
Having to go to a store — prefers to order everything online
Being judged for being a guy who cares about skincare

beliefs: Skincare is a health habit, not a vanity thing — like brushing your teeth
Data and peer reviews beat marketing claims every time
The best routine is the simplest one you'll actually stick to
It's never too late to start taking care of your skin
skills: Comparing products by active ingredient concentration and price per ml
Building spreadsheets to track product performance
Disciplined execution once he commits to a system

general: Spent two weeks reading Reddit threads before buying his first real skincare products
Uses CeraVe cleanser, The Ordinary niacinamide, and a basic moisturizer — that's it
Tracks his routine consistency in a habit-tracking app
Will not buy a product unless it has 4+ stars on Amazon with 1000+ reviews

morning: CeraVe moisturizing lotion, sunscreen on office days
Black coffee and overnight oats at his desk
workday: Standup at 9:30, code review, feature work
Lunch from the office cafeteria or meal-prepped rice bowls
Afternoon bouldering session on WFH days
evening: Cooks dinner — usually something from a recipe he's optimized
Board games online with college friends or solo strategy games
Reads before bed — currently a book on habit formation
weekend: Bouldering at the gym Saturday morning
Works on keyboard builds or side coding projects
Occasional dim sum with his parents in the International District

health.physical: Fit from regular climbing. Started noticing fine lines and some dullness around age 27, which prompted his skincare research. Occasionally gets dry patches from Seattle's winter humidity shifts. Had moderate acne in college that left some mild scarring.
health.mental: Stable and low-stress. Occasionally feels behind compared to peers who started skincare earlier. Approaches personal improvement methodically, which keeps anxiety at bay.

relationships.name: Wei Chen
relationships.description: First-generation Chinese immigrant who has used the same SK-II essence for 20 years. She's thrilled Marcus finally cares about his skin and keeps sending him Korean sheet masks.

relationships.name: David Chen
relationships.description: Civil engineer, also first-gen. Uses nothing on his face. Marcus sees both parents' approaches as data points.

relationships.name: Jenny Lau
relationships.description: UX designer at another tech company. She has an established skincare routine and was the initial catalyst for Marcus looking into it. She finds his spreadsheet approach to skincare adorable.

relationships.name: Ravi Krishnamurthy
relationships.description: Also a developer at Redfin. They climb together 2-3 times a week. Ravi is the one who introduced Marcus to r/SkincareAddiction.
